item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. I was confused on first read about how the Foundation was trying to interfere with them, because I didn't realize on first read that they happened globally. I think that could be clearer. But it definitely got me thinking. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My kind of article. I remember your one about the mirrors at the old folks home; you have a knack for very poignant and subtly disturbing concepts that I’m learning to look forward to.

If I could recommend anything, it would be the footnote about the discovery; go ahead and make it part of the text itself. Also, while the poet in me loves the word “becalmed”, the critic in me thinks it’s way too colorful for this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I always forget the amount vs number distinction. Fixed!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember this from the forums; glad to see it on the mainsite! Really inspires that sort of Series I unsettling dread feeling with comparatively few words.

Quick note though, I think I found a typo:

the improbable amount of coincidences

Should it be "improbable amount of coincidence"? Or "improbably number"? Since "amount" is for stuff that can't be counted, like light or smoke in a room, but number is stuff that can be counted.

Anyway, +1 from me here!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea a lot, but I think the description could be more clear about what it actually is. Specifically I think the phrase "on the surface of the earth" is a bit too vague. I think this is supposed to be interpreted as "across the whole surface of the earth" but I'm really not sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Rather short, but that only adds to the bluntness of the article. It wastes no time getting to the point, which is something new in today's day and age. It would be interesting to see the world in 2055 in this universe, perhaps in a tale.

+1 from me.

item.title: Author post
item.content:encoded:
Musical accompaniment: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JTEFKFiXSx4

Thanks to Reverend Fox, The Great Hippo, Zyn, Kakroom, DarkStuff, and Shanor for reviewing this.
